reviews.text:
Prociutto Pear on Sourdough
The pear was slightly roasted with sugar, very moist and with just the right amount of sweetness. Paired with the salty and fragrant Prociutto, it was unexpectedly delicious. Both of these special drinks （Iced Rose Black Einspenner and Matcha Monster ）are very good. When I ordered, the clerk recommended their latte art to me, saying that the latte art guy in the shop won first place in the Sydney latte art competition. We ordered a labubu and unicorn , and it felt really good and the price was very reasonable

Good coffee good iced matcha

Why not 5 stars ??
Because of multiple things with the food.
The bread on mushroom was a white sourdough and hard as rock, required muscles to break it.
A good chef knows how to serve a softer healthier bread. The mushroom and the cheese were deep fried. Makes it a bad tasting combo and  an unhealthy Choice. The only good thing was poached eggs which i can even make at home. Horrendous experience. My friends plate had horrible tasting vegetables. Salmon didn’t go well with the omelette. Overall 0 i won’t be coming here again

Matcha monster while it has 3 types of matcha, it was super sweet. Almost like a matcha milkshake with how sweet and creamy it was. It's honestly perfect for me, but if you're used to a more bitter and earthy tasting matcha this might be too sweet for you.

Pineapple ham & cheese croissant, a banging sweet and salty combo. Never would've thought to use pineapple jam, but it works . Perfect amount of all 3 ingredients which allowed all the flavours to come through. Croissant was slightly crisp, with a very soft interior. I do wish it was slightly more crisp/toasted though.

Most of you should know my stance on prosciutto & pear in a sandwich. This on sourdough was no different. No honey, but the pears had been roasted/caramelised and was extra sweet which made up for no honey. This did have hollandaise sauce which I'm not the biggest fan of, but it added creamy-ness.

They asked me to try something off their up coming menu and not gonna lie, I was very hesitant. But to my surprise, it was freakin amazing. All the flavours worked soooo well and the charred vegetables had like a deli meat (ham) texture which I ain't mad about. Foccacia was different from others with it being soft and white bread like (?), it didn't have that usual chewy interior.

Korean cafe with some amazing drinks!!! They just released a new Matcha Monster drink which uses 3 types of matcha.

Theyve also got a cherry matcha and I enjoyed the added sourness from it. My fav was the Iced Einspanner which is white coffee with salted hazelnut cream. That cream is SOOOOO GOOD.

Great spot for brunch, they do savoury dishes too like a prawn and salmon roe pasta. Generous prawns with a rich tomato sauce 😋

Full review at @obesi.chi on Insta 😊

Really enjoyed my visit to August Coffee. The dishes were not only delicious but also beautifully plated — fresh ingredients and great flavour combinations. The coffee and matcha drinks were excellent, and the service was very friendly and attentive. The café has a calm, sunlit atmosphere that makes it perfect for brunch or a quiet afternoon catch-up. Highly recommend! 🌸

Lots of tea options here, which I really like. I have tried the sweet ginger tea and the lemongrass ginger on separate visits, the sweet ginger one is my favorite so far.

I also tried their sesame cake and pistachio cake. Pistachio wins for me with mild but clear flavor, chiffon like texture. But the cakes feel overpriced for what they are. I would rather go to another cafe(like Lode or Salt and Light) if im craving dessert or pastry. I will only come back here for the tea, not the cakes.

The cafe is usually full whenever I drop by, so it does get noisy. To be fair, i have never seen it quiet or half empty, so maybe not the best place for reading or working. But it is a good spot to sit down for tea and catch up with a friend.

Service is good, kudos especially the guy who was working around 4 to 5pm on Dec 16, 2025. He is very attentive and kept refilling my hot water without needing to ask.

Tea ceremony vibes. Social place. I will try their matcha next time.

I had such a lovely experience at The Tea Journal and it absolutely deserves a 5/5. From the moment I walked in, the ambience stood out — calm, cosy and thoughtfully designed, making it a perfect place to slow down and actually enjoy your tea. It feels welcoming without being noisy, and there’s a gentle, relaxing vibe that makes you want to stay longer than planned.

The highlight for me was the ginger tea with goji berries. It was beautifully balanced — warm, fragrant and naturally comforting without being overpowering. You can really tell care goes into the preparation, and it felt both refreshing and soothing at the same time. If you enjoy tea that feels intentional rather than rushed, this one is a must-try.

I also had the lychee rose cake and it was delicious. Light, delicate and not overly sweet, with a lovely floral note that paired perfectly with the tea. The texture was soft and fresh, and it felt like a thoughtfully crafted dessert rather than something generic.

Service was superb throughout the visit. Staff were attentive, friendly and genuinely welcoming, creating an experience that felt warm and personal without being intrusive. They were happy to explain menu items and made everything feel easy and relaxed.

Overall, this is a standout spot in Chatswood for tea lovers or anyone wanting a peaceful café experience with high-quality offerings. Great drinks, beautiful desserts, wonderful atmosphere and exceptional service — I’ll definitely be coming back. Highly recommended if you want somewhere calm, refined and genuinely enjoyable.

Lovely spot! Friendly staff and really flavourful iced tea that you can get takeaway or sit in. Lots of teas and beautiful tea cups and tea pots to buy - from dragon pearl green tea, to unique Sichuan bamboo needle tea, and west lake dragon well. Definitely the place to go to buy a special gift for someone who loves tea or a treat for yourself.

Tea Journal finds the perfect balance between modern style and traditional tea culture. We enjoyed pots of Silver Needle and Dragon Pearl, with staff thoughtfully refilling the water after our first pot. We also shared a matcha and strawberry chiffon cake which was delicate and creamy, and paired perfectly with the tea. A relaxed way to enjoy tea with friends.

Really enjoyed our visit to this cosy little tea cafe. The silver jasmine tea was exquisite and the black sesame cake was deliciously light. I loved the fact that the tea was served already decanted into a double walled glass vessel and the leaves were left in the pot that was able to be refilled with boiling water if we wished.

Was our first meal from landing. For some reason everything was on the sweet side. Even the mentaiko pasta was on the sweet side - abit like in Korea where even garlic bread is sweet 😂

Salmon ochazuke was also a tad sweet as was our coffees. Tho I did order skim milk so not sure if that had anything to do with it. Was bit disappointing but not terrible all in all. Spent about AUD$63 for 2

I think there was some japanese dashi like flavoring in their scrambled? It was a nice touch that kept it from being bland without going overboard with the ingredients like some other breakfast places. Classy.

I also like how they steamed the chocolate into the  foam for the soy mocha. I went with large size with extra shot and it just just right, might have been a bit weak if not for the extra shot.

Such a great little coffee spot in Haymarket. Edition Roasters does really good coffee, smooth, well made, and clearly crafted by people who know what they’re doing. The space is cozy and relaxed, perfect for a quick stop or a short sit-down. Staff are friendly and welcoming, which makes it even better. An easy go-to if you’re around the area and want quality coffee without the fuss.

I’ve been here a few times, but I always seem to forget to leave a review.
I just had some magic coffee and banana bread today, and I was chatting with a mate.
The coffee is dead set awesome as always.
The banana bread with yuzu custard was pretty amazing too.
Note:
Came again today during lunch for my dessert.
Tiramisu is a must ordered, and it paired well with magic coffee

I’ve been a regular matcha girlie at Edition Roasters, their matcha is always my fave! Tried the hojicha latte for the first time and it was really good too. The matcha soufflé pancake was soft and fluffy, and the other dish was okay, just a bit on the pricey side. Still a lovely spot overall!

Stumbled on this cafe randomly after dinner and turned out to be such a great experience!

The staff were so kind and welcoming from the moment I walked in. There were so many available seats and also had free wifi🙏😻 I also tried one of their cookies with the iced green tea latte and both were genuinely so good. Everything tasted high quality and made with care. This is definitely a spot I’ll be coming back to, highly recommend if you’re looking for great service and something sweet to enjoy in Lidcombe!

my go-to study cafe! spacious upstairs with seating aswell which is very convenient. the food and drinks are always yummy! they have a new dubai choc drink that isn’t too sweet with fun crunchy pistachio kataifi - give it a try!

I went to this amazing cafe close to Lidcombe station last week and experienced the best customer service! Yellow Days cafe is also a great place to get some study in. The waitress was very kind and made my iced matcha latte which was pretty good. The bathroom was clean and organised which made my experience even better. I would recommend this place to anyone who wants to have a bite and is in the area! They also have the new dubai chocolate drink which I also ordered for my ride home!

Such a lovely café! The atmosphere is perfect for studying or having a chat, super cozy and comfortable. The cookies, coffee, and cakes are all absolutely delicious. Everything tastes amazing, and it’s a place you just want to stay longer. Highly recommend! ☕🍪🍰

Such a lovely cute cafe with a quiet upstairs study area! They have a good selection of cookies and cronuts. Also tried their bulgogi toast and it was great! Here’s a photo of their hibiscus iced tea and grapefruit ade 😋

For me the coffee was good. The mushroom on toast was yummy it had lots of different mushrooms. 😋
The view of watching trains and people was really good because I was enjoying my time on the second level. The service for me was of a good level. To me It had a happy vibe.

I have tried their earl grey cake and the butter scotch latte. Love the latte, the jelly of the earl grey cake is too sweet, but it’s nice once you take that off.
Their pastries look a bit meh and not fresh to me, but the cakes and tarts are worth to try.
Good cafe to hang out after meal.

Btw they don’t list out the price of cakes and pastries.

We had watermelon crushes, decaf iced long blacks, and a decaf latte — all spot on (rare for decaf!). The kids loved the crush. For dessert, we went back to our favourites: blueberry cheesecake, strawberry tart, and the financier. All were just as good, so good they were half gone before I remembered to take photos — the financier is still the winner for me! The vibe was relaxed and quiet enough to enjoy conversations, which made it even better.

I ordered Bunker Brekkie and banana & mango smoothie they charged me $50 and I waited for almost 30 minutes. The food was tasteless, mushroom was smelling very bad like a stale mushroom, eggs were not cooked,  avocado was also rotten and smoothie was too sweet no taste didn’t look fresh.

Had a great breakfast here and was impressed by how good everything was. The food came out quickly and tasted amazing, with everyone enjoying their meal. The Butter Scoth latte was a highlight for me — smooth, well balanced, and comforting. Definitely a café worth checking out.

Special mention to the owners as well.
Thank you for your great service today👍perfect start to our Christmas break!!

Nice place to chill with good coffee and yummy cakes, buns and pastries!

Had einspanner cold brew coffee and gotta say the cold brew is quite special. Cakes slices are slightly on the higher side, eg a slice of plain cheesecake is $10.20 whereas mostly this is priced around $8-9 in Sydney. Does taste very decent though!

Buns and pastries look amazing, very wide selections. Would love to try the macarons someday, they look very unique! Will be back for more.

I bought this cake this morning and was shocked to find grey mold inside the cake, including a strawberry that was clearly not fresh. This is a serious hygiene and food safety issue. Mold does not appear overnight, so this cake was clearly not fresh or was stored improperly. Absolutely unacceptable. I will not be returning and do not recommend this place.

Our go to place for coffee and place to meet up. It has good range of snacks and my personal favourite is their tiramisu and all their cakes with coffee. If you are around its definitely worth a stop.

Love this place! The coffee at LAB is always smooth and perfectly balanced — you can really taste the quality of the beans. The baristas are super friendly and know their craft well. The vibe is calm and modern, great for working or catching up with friends. Definitely one of my favorite coffee spots in town.

Lab in Rhodes is an absolute gem for anyone with a sweet tooth — their baby cream puffs are truly to die for. Each bite bursts with light, airy choux pastry and a perfectly balanced creamy filling that’s both rich and refreshing without being overly sweet. They’re made fresh, so you can taste that just-baked quality with every piece.

The service is another standout. The staff are warm, patient, and always happy to explain the menu or recommend their latest seasonal flavours. Even when it’s busy, the team stays attentive — greeting you with genuine smiles and keeping the vibe calm and inviting. Orders come out quickly, and presentation is always immaculate, with everything plated beautifully or neatly boxed if you’re taking it to go.

The atmosphere matches the desserts: modern, clean, and relaxed — perfect for a casual date, family treat, or coffee catch-up. If you’re in Rhodes and haven’t tried their baby cream puffs yet, do yourself a favour — it’s easily one of the sweetest highlights in the area.

Great range of cakes and buns here, both sweet and savoury, I love Asian style desserts this is why this cafe caught my eye.

I tried the pizza hotdog, pretty much exactly what you’d expect, no surprises there. The coffee, unfortunately, was the let-down for me. It tasted quite burnt, and even as someone who usually drinks it black, I ended up adding sugar just to make it drinkable. A simple long black was $5, which feels a bit steep compared to plenty of cafés closer to the city.
That said, I’d come back to sample more of the cakes, because the selection is genuinely good and tempting.

Today we visited LAB Coffee with our child and ordered three different cakes. However, just like last time, all three cakes were served together on one small plate.

I really didn’t understand why they were combined like that. When three different people order three different cakes, it would be much better, cleaner, and more comfortable if each cake was served on its own plate.

If this is their usual serving style, it may be inconvenient for customers. I hope they can improve this in the future.

Thank you.

Visited LAB in Strathfield and the space feels modern and bright with a lot of seating. The display cabinets are packed with pastries, sliced cakes and mochi. There is a huge range to choose from which makes it fun to browse, almost like walking into a dessert showroom.

I tried the ube sponge cake and the mango mousse cake. The ube cake was soft and light with a mild flavour with a hit of cream. The mango yogurt was refreshing and not too sweet. The fruit was fresh and the yogurt base had a creamy texture without being heavy.

This place is great for choosing pastries to take home or for a late night dessert catch up. There is plenty of seating, the lighting is nice and service at the counter was quick. Prices are on the higher side but the variety and presentation make it worth trying, especially if you like Asian style cakes that are fluffier and less sugary.

Would return to try the bingsu, bread section and more of their mochi.

I just left the café and I feel quite disappointed.

When I ordered, I saw a sign that said “green tea,” but I actually wanted matcha. I asked the staff again to make sure they had matcha, because I know green tea and matcha are not the same. The staff told me they had matcha, so I ordered a regular matcha latte with a cheesecake, as I think they go well together.

While I was waiting, I saw the barista pumping what looked like green tea syrup and then adding milk. I wasn’t sure, but I assumed it wasn’t my drink. In the end, they told me that was mine. After I took a sip, I asked again if it was matcha or green tea. They told me it was green tea.

I explained that I ordered a matcha latte, not green tea. The barista said they are the same, but they are not. Matcha and green tea are different, and that difference is important to customers who specifically ask for matcha.

I just wanted a proper matcha latte in the morning. I hope the staff can learn the difference between matcha and green tea to avoid confusion in the future.

A good place to spend an afternoon with a cup of coffee and a piece of cake. There are plenty of customers who sit here for hours and focus on their own work or task here. Come early to secure a good seat. It's not very noisy and one can talk without difficulty. Just that there is no free wifi provided by the restaurant.

Excellent Coffee and cakes. I got small $5 walnut bite cakes and Papa bun and small tea.m cake. The service is top notch and coffee is perfect. We got long black and latte. They also have a QR code for review and they give free Cream puff for review. The overall ambiance is great. I forgot to take pics initially so apologies for not so good images attached. One small suggestion is to provide some power points under and / or near seating for laptop and / mobile charging. There is one power point though  it away from the seating.

The Cream puff taste so good because the cream is so creamy and  so rich. And the custard bun is yummy so creamy so heavenly. The ice latte is rich in coffee. In total I spent close to $20 Aussie dolllars but I truly in love with it.

The service staff was very nice to me she answered to my question when I ask whether do we still get the free cream puff after we fill the Google review and she even offer help to get the cream puff after I showed her the Google review. Thank you so much for your assistance.

Overall I love the taste of the cream of all the pastry and I also find the ambience of the place quite cosy and not noisy and I can do my work and reading there without any loud disturbance from the surrounding.

Loved the vibe at LAB Parramatta! The food was beautifully presented and tasted amazing! super fresh and full of flavour. The cocktails were creative and perfectly balanced. Service was friendly and quick too. Definitely a great spot for brunch or a night out

I usually enjoy coming here for both the Papparoti and the Einspanner. However, today the bun didn’t have the usual coffee aroma and buttery flavor, and the inside was dense and dry. The bun was almost like regular bread. The Einspanner also didn’t taste as good as it usually does.
I visit this café very frequently and usually enjoy the coffee here. However, this time I was truly disappointed

This was an absolutely unacceptable experience. We waited 20 minutes for a simple frappe, only to be told — after we followed up — that they were out of whipped cream and couldn’t make it at all.

A refund doesn’t fix the fact that we wasted 20 minutes waiting for something the staff should have known and communicated from the start. No proactive update, no basic courtesy, nothing.
Had a lemon iced tea and tiramisu cup. The best tiramisu I had so far. Iced tea was pretty good too. Loved the service from the female barista, thank you guys!

Good coffee. I feel like they need to work on Wagyu burger, they can make it a bit flavourful. My friend ordered pumpkin soup, it felt like the onion over powered. The staff was a bit confused when we asked if the food was halal, and then confirmed with the manager.

I was surprised and couldn’t believe in the way you packed your online order. The cup’s straw opening was left completely unsealed, yet still got placed in the bag without any protection. This caused the coffee to spill over everything. The entire order ended up soaked.

This is the worse packing I’ve ever seen in Sydney. Could you please be more professional and make sure the drinks are properly sealed with your future customers?

Loved this place! The coffee and chai were excellent, and the açai bowl was spot on — perfectly balanced and not overly sweet. Such a great spot for a relaxed catch-up with friends or family. Warm, easygoing vibe and somewhere I’d happily return to again and again.

Came to this cafe to have lunch. The food was very tasty and the portion was also good. The staff were very welcoming and the especially the barista was very experienced and friendly. She made some excellent coffees for us. Loved the experience !!

editorial_summary.overview: Bright, funky space with corrugated walls and red stools, making sandwiches and specialty coffee.

reviews.text:
We just had lunch at The Wedge, it's our favourite café in Glebe for a quick bite and coffee. It’s always a great spot to relax and refuel.

The chicken sambo is hands down our favourite thing on the menu! It's consistently fresh, tasty and super satisfying. My son is a big fan of the ham and cheese croissant, which never lasts long once it hits the table.

Great coffee, friendly service and owners and a reliable go-to in the neighbourhood. Highly recommended!

As someone who works in hospitality for almost 10 years, just want to give a genuine review to this shop as it was below my expectations.

We got here without knowing that reservation was needed to get seated and the main guy (probably the owner or manager) seated us kindly. We were seated by the window on the ground floor.

We waited for a bit but noone came to tell us its table service, so I went up checking on the baristas if its table service as noone came back to check with us for drinks. I asked the baristas if its table service and they werent even friendly when they answered to me its table service. Head back to the table, waited for the staff to come and take our order.

It took more than 15 minutes for our coffee to came out and more than 20 minutes for the pastries and more than 20 minutes for a smoothie.

Food and drinks service was very slow, coffee was average and no presentation on the drinks. I was going to try the pastries and wait for a bit before order some lunch for us but we were disappointed.

They have good selection of pastries and food, nice seatings and location, friendly waitstaff, but I’m not sure if i’d come back to here.

Maybe it was us and our expectations 😅

I recently visited The Wedge in Glebe for breakfast and ordered the Lisbon Breakfast. The café has a pleasant atmosphere. I sat by the window, which was nice for the natural light, although the view wasn’t ideal because a couple of cars were parked right outside. That part isn’t within the café’s control, but it’s worth keeping in mind if you expect a scenic outlook.

The service was great. My order came out quickly, and the staff were warm, attentive, and checked in regularly without being overbearing.

The food was a new experience for me. It included a poached egg served on a special fish, topped with hollandaise sauce on a hash brown. I enjoy trying new dishes, and this one was genuinely nice. I do think the plate would benefit from some vegetables or a small salad on the side, just to balance the meal a bit more.

I also had a matcha latte. I usually only drink matcha at Korean or Japanese cafés, but I decided to try it here. It was surprisingly good and not too sweet, which I prefer.

Overall, it was a very good experience, and I would recommend The Wedge in Glebe for breakfast. In terms of price, it was reasonable for the area, but considering the portion size, it didn’t feel fully worth it.

I loved everything about the café — the food was refreshing, the vibe was chill, and the staff were all friendly, especially the gorgeous lady in the red top with her beautiful smile and great service. If I lived in the area, it would definitely be my go-to café. Not to mention, it’s very kid-friendly.

In a city with booming cafe culture the Wedge Glebe stands out. It’s colourful, elegant, art filled interior, alongside the curated euro jazz playlists create a vibe that’s not to be missed. The food on the unique menu always hits the spot and the friendly staff are ready and willing to help you out with any questions or requests you might have. During my latest visit to the Wedge with my Dad, we enjoyed the Roasted Veg Mixed Grain Salad with the incredible house smoked trout which keeps me coming back again and again. For dessert we had the house made fruit and nut loaf which was a delicious treat to finish the meal. Of course the coffee which they source from Single O is amazing too!
You can’t go wrong at The Wedge 😊
(p.s. the photo is of their delicious cheesecake which is also incredible!)

I recently visited Social Butterfly Tea & Dessert and had such a lovely experience. The atmosphere is truly beautiful — calm, peaceful, and thoughtfully designed. It’s the kind of place where you can relax and enjoy your time without feeling rushed.

The tea selection is wonderful, especially the green tea, which was fragrant and perfectly balanced. The cakes were also delicious. Everything felt carefully prepared and presented with attention to detail.

The service was excellent at every level. The staff were warm, attentive, and genuinely welcoming, which made the visit even more enjoyable.

It’s a perfect spot for working quietly, reading a book, or having a relaxed conversation with friends. I would highly recommend this place to anyone looking for a cozy and peaceful tea experience.

Thanks Casper for a fantastic night. We had a long day of events this Saturday and were drawn in by a sign out in front leading to a lift - well, let’s just say that paved a way to something very interesting.

I’m only just getting into tea now after practically being a soft drink and water person, and I was really impressed by the thorough craft of the caterers and the gentle service provided by the staff, it was a wonderful experience and I’d love to come back. In saying that, the dessert and tea quality was brilliant and pleased the taste buds with a balance across bitter and sweet; I really liked it.

The overall atmosphere was lovely and lowkey, it felt like a great place to wind down and I’d definitely entertain the idea of coming here after a long day or a post-work wind down during the week. Great music, good ambiance, quiet chatter. Casper (or Kasper if it’s with a K!) was kind enough to entertain our conversation and tell us his brief story of coming down to Sydney - thanks for sharing and welcome in mate!

Apparently, this place only opened up 2 weeks ago and I strongly believe it’s off to a strong start. It’s a little quiet and quaint, and while I do wish for great success in the future, I’m glad it’s all our little secret for now as it’ll definitely see great patronage ahead. Thanks team for your warm smiles, warm tea and lovely service.

Would I come back? Yep.

It was such a cozy atmosphere here, we have never experience a cafe in Sydney like this! This place has its own magic. The owner and the team is very welcoming and they will explain what teas suits you, and their cold brew tea is wonderful! Not too sweet, and their dessert is WONDERFUL! Especially the cloud cake!

Will definitely visit again!

It’s a lovely place that’s perfect for reading, working, or going on a date. The atmosphere is cute and warm, and the service is very friendly. It feels very welcoming—almost like being served by a close friend. If you don’t know much about tea, they are happy to recommend and guide you. ☕✨

Visited on their very first day of opening and had a lovely experience. The space is calm, cozy, and beautifully lit, perfect for a relaxing tea and dessert break.
Desserts and drinks were served quite quickly, which was impressive for an opening day. The flavours were well balanced and not overly sweet, with a fragrant matcha that really stood out.
A great new spot in the city, wishing the team all the best!
Stitch broadway was a better experience than QVB. More spacious and relaxed setting. Loved the chicken sando and tomato bagel.

First breakfast in Sydney! Cream cheese and tomato bagel - 9/10 Garlic cream cheese bagel -10/10 Mushroom toastie - 8/10. Food was absolutely great the highlight being the garlic cream cheese bagel. Had the Bolivian Moose cascara as well and was blown away by the fruity, tea like, flavour. Very refreshing on a hot day like today 🥵

Only been there for coffee in the morning and it was too good. I ordered the seasonal special white rabbit and the strawberry matcha latte which was great!

The white rabbit was a creamy long black with hazelnut flavour and had a dried fruit-like aroma/flavor with slightly sweet finished. It is one of my favorite blend so far. I ended up buying their coffee beans as well

The cafe interior is unique, warm and cozy. Might be a bit crowded in coffee rush hour but pretty sure that everyone can wait for a good coffee. Some staff was friendly but some wasn’t (from what I experienced). However, overall was too good to not coming back.
Really awesome food and lovely staff, great coffee and a nice atmosphere. Cannot fault this place. Highly recommend the steamed egg sando!

So far the best matcha latte in the area. But, the quality comes at a cost - as "warned" by other reviews. Unsweetened matcha option cost $6.50 for a small cup that my diminutive hand (small Asian girl) could wrap around completely. Regular (sweetened powder) matcha cost $6 for same small cup.
The drink itself had strong matcha taste (which I like) and perfect thickness. However, the temperature was not hot enough for me. It was only slightly hotter than lukewarm, IMO. I'd rate it 9.5/10.

Loved their cakes, tried the Earl grey and sesame cakes, they're both fluffy and velvety. Loved their cream puff as well. The strawberry matcha latte drink tastes great too. It looks like a small Bakery from the outside, but was surprised to know they have bigger space at the back. Busy place but still able to talk. Very nice place.

Friendly customer service. I bought their Mango Green Tea Cake Slice. It was worth the price as the fruit was fresh as a topping and the cake was soft. I really enjoyed the combination of flavours. I would definitely return.
P.S. Card surcharge for purchases under $15

The staff are very kind. I had a frustrating experience at another shop earlier and was quite flustered, so I didn’t even think about buying candles. One of your staff members kindly asked me and made sure to include candles for me, which I truly appreciated.

Delicious sweets/cakes and the coffee was excellent too. It was pretty packed at the front, so I was really glad there was extra seating out the back. We will be back and try the other sweets/cakes :)

There are a few Korean-style bakeries in Eastwood, but Lavgjgne is by far my favourite — and in my opinion, much better than the neighbouring ones. They offer amazing breads like the gwaebigi twisted donut and glutinous rice donut, along with many other classics. There's also a lovely outdoor area at the back, perfect for sitting with family and friends. A must-try is their sweet potato cake and blueberry chiffon cake — both are absolutely delicious!

editorial_summary.overview: Hearty breakfasts and cafe bites in a book-lined venue with a living room vibe and laneway seating.

reviews.text: Spent a morning here recently and really loved the atmosphere. It’s set across three floors of books, which gives it a very quiet, cozy vibe—apparently, it was even ranked as one of the most beautiful book-cafés in the world this year. We tried the Turkish eggs and the green bowl; both were fresh and had great flavor, which was a nice surprise as sometimes "themed" cafes can be a bit hit-or-miss with the food. It’s a great little sanctuary in Paddington if you want to eat surrounded by books.

Highly recommended. We had an amazing experience at this café, truly unique and memorable. The staff were wonderful, and the manager, Payam, was exceptionally respectful and professional. When someone mistakenly sat at our reserved table, he handled the situation with outstanding customer service and grace.
The food was absolutely delicious, and we thoroughly enjoyed every bite. When we shared our feedback, we were told the café is ranked number six in the world, which comes as no surprise. Congratulations to the owners, this café truly deserves its place among the best.

reviews.author_name: Aurélie • Boost Yourself / Mova Mova Coaching
reviews.text:
What can I say about this place, except that I had a truly wonderful time there. The coffee is absolutely divine, and the atmosphere — with books spread across several levels — has everything I love to feel inspired, unwind, and think about the growth of my business.

I highly recommend it; the vibes are excellent. And just for the record, the beef cheek pasta is a must-try — absolutely incredible.
The Turkish eggs had a lot of labneh but the flavour was kind of flat. Maybe a bit of acidity would’ve helped. The outside area had a nice breeze. The upstairs was pretty cool.

Still the best spot for a halloumi sandwich. Pear, passion fruit & ricotta french toast isn’t really my thing, but it was fresh and nicely done.

A bit on the pricey side, but you could say it’s worth it for the taste and I guess that’s just Sydney being Sydney.

You couldn’t ask for much more from a cafe. Don’t be deterred by pre-prepared food, because it was excellent. I had the chicken salad and my partner had one of the toasties and it tasted as though it had been prepared then and there, not to mention when I couldn’t finish it, it was quick and easy to take with me! On top of that, the cold and warm drinks menu was both vast and delicious; rarely can I go to a cafe with my coffee loving partner and get something that I took thing is delicious. My partner is hard to please with coffee and thought their cold brew was excellent, and I was hooked from the first sip of my iced matcha latte.
The place has a great vibe too. Casual without being devoid of character, music without that somehow both doesn’t take over the conversation but it also very cool, and so many seating options to suit whatever mood you’re in (especially offering plenty of outdoor and seating aswell).
The service was friendly without overcompensating and time wasting, drinks and food both came out super fast despite it being busy, and on that, even though it was at full capacity indoors, it didn’t feel suffocatingly busy like some cafes do.
We have a weekly activity here and like to try new cafes every Saturday morning, but I can’t imagine us going anywhere else now.

Oh and last but not least they have a cute little smattering of gift shop types items you can purchase such as coffee beans, card games, all of which are beautifully displayed too.

Give it a try, you won’t regret it.

We absolutely love the overall menu at La Lune Café and visit often because it never disappoints.

One thing we especially enjoy is the coffee — it has such a distinct and beautiful flavour. As coffee lovers, we really appreciate how unique and well-crafted it is.

And every time we have the moist, fluffy scones or the bagel with spring-onion cream cheese, it genuinely fills us with happiness. The freshness and quality here are on a different level, and it always leaves us delighted.

Thank you for creating such a wonderful experience every time.

This is one of my top cafes in Sydney! I ALWAYS order the mango smoothie (one of the best in Sydney I'd say), and all of the croffles are super tasty. The tiramisu is a standout with the cream and fluffiness of the croffle tasting so good. My favourite part is definitely the atmosphere and seating! Inside feels like you're in South Korea, and is beautifully and comfortably designed!
Scone is very good and I'd consider the price to be quite friendly. Small coffee $5.5 is a bit high but it's good coffee. Nice vibe. Very quick service.

Cute little cafe in North Strathfield serving Korean inspired foods and drinks! We had the Iced Matcha, Iced Banana Cold Brew and a full Ham Sandwich. Nothing really special about the sandwich. Iced Matcha was quite milky and the matcha taste was quite weak. Iced Banana Cold Brew was special and good though. Tried their basque cheesecake before and it was good!

We went there for breakfast. The service was good and generous. The price was reasonable. We ordered big breakfast and the smash avocado, they tasted good, however the sausage tasted weird. I would suggest adding the spinach or avocado on the big breakfast. The cafe has a garden setting with a fish pond, very nice and relaxing.

beautiful laid-back cafe with koi fish pond very easy to read menu nice staff, nicely made cappuccino/ good coffee blend. Pet friendly and lots of seating space. Indoor and outdoor seating. Able to help yourself to table water. Only downside was the price of the meals and what you are getting/quality. Wasn’t bad quality just was expecting more.

I moved to this neighbourhood a year ago and have often visited for the beautiful space and good food. Unfortunately, the service has consistently fallen short. Today, when my husband noticed the pancakes were different from usual and we politely asked about it, we felt dismissed by a comment suggesting we might not know the difference between pancakes and crêpes. I value service as much as food, and repeated cold experiences have made us feel unwelcome.

Enjoying one of my favourite places for breakfast while walking my dog. This cafe is pet friendly environment as my dog knows the way to stop 🛑 😂. Relaxing atmosphere with high quality of customers. Amazing food selection available for families gathering as this cafe has a quiet corner with few tables for those to study or businesses meeting.

Aside from the mine and my Mother’s meals coming out 15-minutes apart, fantastic experience.

Not dined here in many, many years but glad that we did for lunch today.
Coffee is always brilliant, and the Barramundi was absolutely fantastic - full sized fillet, cooked perfectly with fantastic fresh sides.

Will be back for more than coffee!

It’s such a strange yet lovely place. I really like the atmosphere here and the matcha too. The café isn’t crowded, there are just a few seats, and it’s not noisy at all, which makes it perfect for chatting. I said “strange” because it’s both a café and a clothing store, but I actually love that! You can sip your drink while checking out cool fashion pieces at the same time.

Found this cafe somewhere hidden behind all tall buildings in the city.

Tried my luck, ordered a a normal hot long black, cold brew and a special “Moon River” to try.

Good beans, nice environment, very muji.
Such a fancy spot! Loved their matcha latte and cold brew. The vibe here is so nice — I’ll definitely be back!

I hop around cafes but come here regularly as an office worker. Truly one of the best coffee first cafes in all of Sydney. The coffee is always made perfectly that is a flat white is made flat instead of foamy. They change up the beans so you can sample from different roasters and a well made teas and iced coffees too.
Coffee quality is just so amazing. I was shocked. The chairs are not very comfy but would love to come

Best crepes, best strawberry matcha I’ve ever had so far. The crepes are freshly made, so soft and creamy, not too sweet. The drinks are consistently delicious. The shop has a cozy, welcoming vibe. The staffs are lovely ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Bubble Nini was an absolute hit at our wedding and Lara was wonderful to work with from start to finish. The bubble tea was fresh, delicious and exactly what everyone needed on a hot day.  'Sun & Moon' was a hit throughout and our guests loved how Bubble Nini was just that extra bit luxurious as a bubble tea.

Bubble Nini added such a fun and refreshing touch to our celebration and we couldn’t recommend them more. We’ve always loved their bubble tea casually and it was just as perfect as part of our wedding celebration as well.

Lovely little cafe with great vibes ☺️

I ordered a matcha drink and it came with a small flower on the table — such a simple but thoughtful touch. I brought this back for my wife and she was genuinely so happy, it really made her day.

Drinks were delicious, presentation was beautiful, and the atmosphere was warm and cosy. You can tell they care about the little details.

Highly recommend if you’re after good drinks and a nice experience 🌼💚

Fantastic choices and great hours!

This place has an awesome drinks menu (brown sugar matcha is recommended) and the cakes are so good but u need to get in earlier as they will run out.

Nice spot for a date night or dessert to cap off the day

A small cafe in Zetland. Good ambience, humble staff, lovely matcha drink, and cake. The boba was not to my taste; it was not chewy. The pistachio drink was also not to my taste; it was plain and had no taste or smell of pistachio.

The service and atmosphere at Berry Boy Açai was fantastic. We travelled all the way from the south west of Sydney after seeing them all over the socials and it wasn’t a disappointment. I was pleased with the recommendation and glad we drove out to Hurstville to try it. We will definitely be back.

Absolutely love this place!
The portion sizes are so generous – definitely worth every dollar. The açaí was fresh, texture was on point, and they don’t skimp on the sauce (which I really appreciate!). Great value for money, and I left feeling satisfied from my late night dessert run. Highly recommend!!
DELICIOUS. The best 😄 the facility needs air conditioning for hot days. But the açai is on point.

The Mog Champ sends his regards.

Came here for dessert after having yum cha for brunch and it was definitely worth skipping out on the sweets earlier.  Ordered a baby boy (which would be their medium-sized cup) with acai, blueberries and pistachio sauce which was great!

Place is nicely decorated, it's got a fun atmosphere especially with their in-house photo booth setup as well that people are free to enjoy. Service was friendly and fast. I'm interested in their Acai Snack Pack which I saw from the sign at the counter. Maybe next time. Thanks Berry Boy!

Mogliest Regards,
Your Mog Champ

🔥 Just had the BEST acai bowl at Berry Boy Acai in Hurstville! 🙌 Tried to grab a size tonight and every other spot was shut, but this place saved me 🦸‍♂️. The assigh (loving the Aussie slang 😂) was ON POINT - super tasty and exactly what I needed! 😋 Will def be back with mates. Cheers for keeping it 🔥, Berry Boy Acai! 👌"

Obsessed with this café’s vibe—soft lighting, cozy seating, and such a calm, welcoming atmosphere! Their latte is creamy with a rich coffee flavor that hits just right. Service is friendly, and it’s the perfect spot to unwind with a drink. Highly recommend for coffee lovers!

An absolutely fantastic brunch spot! I’m especially in love with their Iced Earl Grey Latte — it’s incredibly smooth and fragrant, with a perfectly balanced tea aroma that’s simply addictive. The brunch menu offers a great variety of both Chinese and Western dishes, so there’s something for everyone! My favorite is the Chinese Breakfast Set B: youtiao (fried dough), tea egg, and doufunao (tofu pudding). It tastes just like home! For brunch, I always pick their Chicken Mushroom Risotto. Whenever I can’t decide where to go for lunch, coming here is always the right choice. Cozy atmosphere and friendly service — definitely my go-to weekend spot!

My weekend morning haunt after gym.
The best Ham and Cheese croissant in town! Absolutely love them! Beautiful Taro Lattes as well.
Staff are always friendly and always remember me even though I only go once a week!
Really nice cafe with a chill vibe. Coffee was good and the food came out fresh. Good service and friendly staff made the whole experience comfortable. Would come back again!!!!

I don’t usually leave negative reviews, but this experience was disappointing.
my 7-year-old daughter and ordered a thick shake, not a milkshake. I was charged an extra $3 for it, which I was fine with. However, when it arrived, it was still very much a milkshake—thin and runny.
I politely asked if more ice cream could be added and re-blended. I even said I was happy to pay extra again to get it right. The response I received was simply, “Sorry, this is how we do it.”
I explained that a thick shake means more ice cream and less milk—it’s not complicated. I was told one extra scoop had already been added (which is what the $3 was for), but it still wasn’t a thick shake. I again offered to pay for additional scoops, and the request was refused.
What frustrated me most wasn’t the money—it was the lack of flexibility or willingness to help, especially when I was polite, patient, and with a young child.
I usually go to the café a few doors down that serves Campos coffee, but they were very busy that day, so I decided to give this place a try. Unfortunately, this experience showed me why customer service and listening matter so much for small businesses.
I wasn’t asking for anything unreasonable—just for what I paid for.

I tried Bingsu at this dessert store and based on my experience, I will vote 3 stars as nothing special. I tried 2 favours (strawberry and matcha), but the taste is really the same, just colour different. The fruit topping was not fresh as well.
Won't come back for Bingsu.

I came here around in January and ordered a Korean egg drop from the menu. Unfortunately, it seems there has been changes of the chief and he didn’t know how to made it and served something like a burger / beckon and egg roll. I explained to the front staff for what is egg drop sandwich and how the egg should be mixed. They said they got it and  and did it a second time however nothing seemed changed except for they added a different sauce. They should remove things from the menu or let customers know if they are not available or don’t know how to make it.

We ordered green tea bingsu.

Very bland flavour. They just dust green tea powder on top of vanilla ice cream which other shops they serve the actual green tea ice cream. The bingsu is vanilla flavour as well. Not green tea. Quite disappointed.

Cheese cake and chocolate cake are very dense.

Not awful but not amazing either. I've been here a few times and the reviews and rating does reflect what you get here. A while back, the taro bingsu I tried tasted weird and was filled with a bunch of odd ingredients nor did the actual bingsu itself taste much of taro. The cakes are dry and bland like they've been kept in the display case longer than they should've.
Recently we tried the mango bingsu which we had mixed feelings about. Why isn't the ice infused with mango flavouring and why aren't fresh mangoes used? Frozen mangoes are used instead and the bingsu is a basic milk flavour. That being said, it didn't taste terrible but it's pushing it for $19. I'd pay max 15. Also, was bit pissed to see afterwards in other reviews that it was actually supposed to come with a cheesecake slice but we weren't given that nor was an explanation given as to why - I don't care if I miss out so long as you tell us.
It'd be a place I'd go to if the queues for other bingsu places are long and I'm feeling desperate. The vibes of the cafe are great and it's a nice place to sit down with friends

Dessert store in Strathfield that offers drinks and bingsu, cakes.
We tried soy bean bingsu and tiramisu bingsu. Both were pretty bad, not tasty at all. No flavour. They were just ice shaved with cereal and ice cream basically for all the bingsu. Worst bingsu I have ever had unfortunately. Definitely wont be back and not recommended!
I really enjoyed the big breakfast at Sunbrew Cafe. The food was fresh, well-cooked, and very filling. Great service and a nice atmosphere too. Definitely worth a visit!

What a gem!!! Tucked away on a side street in Narwee, this cafe is a true gem. The coffee is excellent, the service warm and welcoming, and the food absolutely outstanding. The crispy chills eggs is a must-try—perfectly executed with delicious bread. We’ll definitely return to explore more of the menu. With its delicious food, friendly staff, and reasonable prices, this is hands down the best local cafe in the area. They also serve pepe saya butter with their toast and showcase Sonoma baked goods, bringing a touch of the East and Inner West’s quality without the need for travel. Highly recommend this spot for both coffee and meals! I just wish if they have halal meat or other pork offering on the breakfast menu.

A lovely cafe located down a side street in Narwee. The coffee is great, the service is friendly, and the food is expectational. Highly recommend the eggs bene, so good and the bread is delicious. We'll definitely be back to try other dishes on the menu.

Delicious food, friendly staff, reasonable prices - the best local Cafe in the area! They've got pepe saya butter for their toast and sonoma baked goods in the display, which is honestly the best without needing to travel all the way to the East or inner west for the fancier cafes. Couldn't recommend this place more for their coffee and meals!
Nice atmosphere and friendly staff. Good quality coffee with professional baristas. Highly recommended the bacon and egg wrap.

We ordered gelato, chocolate bingsu, the flan and the coconut matcha. The flan was big enough to serve 2/3 and came with toppings on the side (boba balls and shaved ice). It was delicious and not too sweet. The bingsu serving size fed 5 of us and had so many different, delicious toppings. Highly recommend! The matcha wasn’t such a hit though since it was quite bitter. The vibe was nice, a small cafe with soft music and dimmed lights. Overall an 8/10 experience

Terrible customer experience. I had contacted the store after leaving behind a bag of food and had just wanted to know if it was still there or not so I could return and pick it up.

The male employee who picked up was extremely rude and stated “no” without checking and hung up. Contacted them once more and didn’t get to speak much before they hung up again.

After contacting the store for a third time, the male employee stated that he was “too busy” check and didn’t want to take a look outside. The store was mostly empty when we left so I was confused as to why they couldn’t quickly check and provide a proper answer.

Whether the food was still there or not, I wouldn’t even be sure since I wasn’t given a proper answer nor any assurance.

The Fruit chunks with durian was also made with fruit that wasn’t fresh so it left a weird after taste. Overall, the terrible customer service will be a reason not to return.

Very interesting and nice desserts with a Vietnamese and Korean flavour. The iced desserts are delicious, my favourite being the matcha flavour. The Vietnamese egg coffee was very cute, with an interesting flavour and very strong coffee! The complementary cold tea was also very refreshing. Wish they would be more environmentally friendly and stop using plastic spoons especially when dining in.

It was an extremely disappointing experience.

It was a busy night and our group (two families) had to sit at two separate tables. About 15 minutes after we arrived, a male staff member asked our smaller table to leave, even though we had just sat down and were still eating. We were told to finish up with our dessert so another group could take the table. Meanwhile, the other table in our group was still eating. This felt very unfair and uncomfortable.

I didn’t want to create a scene, so I let it go. However, things got worse when our kids at the other table found a fly in their mango bingsu. When we raised the issue, staff offered to replace it, but our kids were already upset and completely put off after seeing an insect in their food. They didn’t want to eat there anymore.

Despite us clearly saying no, the staff kept insisting on making another bingsu. We had to raise our voices to explain that it would just go to waste and that we no longer felt comfortable staying after the whole experience.

In the end, we were offered a $10 voucher, which honestly felt pointless as we have no intention of returning.

Overall, poor handling of seating, hygiene concerns, and a lack of empathy for customers  especially families with children. Very disappointing

The bingsu is pretty good for the area. Good to have a good bingsu place in the area rather than a trip to Strathfield!
The Mango purple rice yoghurt is also pretty good. Highly recommend the Matcha Bingsu. The brown sugar flan isnt that great.
It is also reasonably priced.
Definately worth a visit!

10/10 would recommend. Such lovely service. And the shakshuka eggs were amazing! The only tip is to reduce the sugar slightly.
And the French toast was perfect 👌 such a good balance of soft inside and crispy on the edges. Thank you for a lovely brunch.
It's a small cafe but the coffee was really good. I like the little touches such as the quaint crooked teaspoon!

It is a cute cafe. Located close to mascot station. Ordered a coffee and eggs benedict - at the time it was fine; good, delicious. However went got home, got really sick (it was the pretty much the only thing I ate from that point on for the rest of the day and remained unwell, bathroom wasn’t fun) after eating it… hopefully it was just a one off thing but it certainly felt like food poisoning or similar at the time, that’s unfortunately why a star or two is lost -otherwise service was quick and staff friendly.

The restaurant is simple but the food is delicious.  I had a breakfast burger with bacon, eggs, lettuce, avocado, and tomato relish on a burger bun.  It was perfect for a late hungry breakfast.  I also had an apple/carrot/ ginger juice which was fantastic.  I would highly recommend this place.

Visited for breakfast and ordered some savoury and sweet dishes. Staff were welcoming. Portions were large, food was very well presented and full of flavour. The juice was refreshing. Only downside was the noise from traffic as the cafe is next to a busy road.

We had lunch here a couple of weeks ago. We ordered the creamy pasta and a waffle. The pasta was delicious and the staff were friendly. The downside was that the food took a little while to arrive, and the chocolate on the waffle wasn't the greatest quality, but everything else was good or average.

The café is really lovely and the coffee is absolutely fantastic. I loved how they offer a wide variety of desserts at different price points, and they also serve breakfast. Unfortunately, the kitchen ventilation wasn’t working during my visit, so the entire place was filled with cooking smells. It was quite unpleasant and took away from the otherwise beautiful atmosphere. I hope they fix this issue because the café truly deserves more visits.

Lovely customer service, and friendly people!
Food was great (chicken schnitzel roll), and the coffees are perfect (Iced Latte and large soy cap)

Driving along the main street in Lidcombe, you can see a pink color cafe.   Stepping inside and it has a warm atmosphere.  Ordered three different drinks and they are all above standard.   Very polite staff  and they always wear a welcoming smile and are attentive.    When I left,  I forgot my jacket.    The waitress ran out and brought it back to me.   Hearty thanks to the lady.   Definitely we will be back to try other food.

Very friendly service and atmosphere at this venue. I arrived around 12:15, at the height of the lunchtime rush, despite this I was able to find a small table for one. Ordered a small flat-white coffee,  a sandwich (more about this later), and a slice of cake. $18.20 for dining in,  not bad at all.

The cake and sandwich were in those clear plastic boxes,  so were probably meant for takeaway customers, however,  the member of staff who took my order graciously put these on plates for me. Very kind. All of the items were placed on the table after only a few moments. The coffee was nice, smooth, with a nice taste and good milk to coffee ratio. The sandwich was something of a surprise,  being two halves of two separate sandwiches : salad and egg salad. Nice and fresh, and tasty. The cake was also fresh, I think it was a spongecake with white chocolate.

The vibe here is best described as friendly and casual,  several people were here chatting happily during my visit. I noticed a small stage with microphones,  a piano, and other instruments. Live music must be a feature here, but not during my visit. I walked there from Lidcombe railway station,  it took me approximately 15 minutes.

My favourite local coffee shop & the perfect post-run stop this Xmas morning 🎄🏃‍♀️
I had an iced pure matcha & the tender chicken breast salad bowl. Both were spot on. The matcha was smooth & refreshing. The salad was fresh, light & satisfying, & the chicken breast was beautifully soft & tender (just like the name 😉)
Great coffee, great food, & a lovely local vibe. Always a pleasure coming here - highly recommend!

Good atmosphere for a cafe that’s situated on the main road of Kingsgrove. The interiors for the cafe is quite well maintained and nice.

Unfortunately though there’s about where the good things end.

I ordered the chicken salad, which came with veggies that weren’t fresh. Browning of the greens was pretty off setting.

Chicken was cooked to perfection I’ll give them that. But there was no real seasoning. And the photos on the sign board were a bit misleading as it showed two poached eggs which didn’t come with me. (I guess you’d have to order to the add-ons for that)

The cafe is surprisingly busy as well. But for my own preference the coffee tasted a bit weak for me. You may like it if you prefer a slightly lighter roast.

Should also mention that tables were seated on was also dirty and unclean. Had to request for staff to give it a wipe down. (Not the end of the world)

Overall, I’m giving the place the benefit of the doubt. Maybe the main head chef was on holiday leave and I simply got unlucky? 🤷‍♂️

Personally though I expected more.

We always go to Ickle at least once a week. Honestly, the coffee here is one of the best in Sydney! The team there are always so nice and friendly. It is a dog friendly cafe, and we love that! They serve great food too. Our favourite ones are their avocado toast, congee and kimchi toast :) highly recommended this place.
Perfect matcha latte. Great place to try if you are in the Kingsgrove area when visiting Sydney. The staff are always friendly and the food and drink always taste amazing.

real hidden gem!!💎
I found on google map and actually didn't expect this much but everything was perfect.
especially coffee is the best in Sydney!

Perfect coffee and food. Staff are lovely and very friendly. I ordered the bagel . it was very good. The bagel was crispy and very tasty. I highly recommend all the bagels and coffee. They are really good.
The food and coffee here are so good! I’ve come back several times . Especially for the oat mocha, I’m obsessed with it!.

Haven’t been to a cafe for a long time and always want to check this place out. Very cute atmosphere, lots of plants. We sat in the garden at the back. So relaxing. We ordered chicken caesar salad with spicy dressing and ice latte. We finished with salted caramel muffin and lemon muffin. Delicious! Perfect late lunch for two. Amy, the owner is super friendly.
Nice cozy café. Bagel for 9 dollar which is hard to find around oxford street.
Great staff and lovely cafe. My wife loves their gluten free french toast. Their bagel sandwiches and bibimbap are fantastic. They make really good coffee. Highly recommended.
Fantastic little cozy cafe by the park. Serving very delicious cafe food. The tater tots and milk shakes are amongst the must orders. Service was speedy and staff were very friendly and helpful!

Had a mix-up with my order at Redroof Cafe — it wasn’t sent to the kitchen, so we waited over 30 minutes. But the staff and owner handled it well then, with sincere apologies and free snacks, coffees to make up for it. The food was absolutely delicious, and their attitude was warm and genuine. Great service recovery and truly worth a 5-star review!

- Friendly customer service, staff are always polite and accommodating. Service can be a little slow sometimes.
- Dog friendly
- Nice decor with the staghorn ferns & cute budgerigars
- Cool shelter for hot days
- Coffee is good
- Prices are reasonable (for Sydney), when I was last there they did a toastie and coffee deal for about $12
- Toilet at the back (you need to turn on the tap below the basin to allow water flow).

My husband and I have breakfast here often, and we absolutely love it! Whenever we go for a walk, we always make sure to detour so we can at least sneak in a coffee. Today we were served by our usual guy’s wife, and she was so lovely and friendly that I felt compelled to write a review. Such a welcoming little café! 11/10 recommend ☕😊

Quality undercover outdoor cafe located on the Homebush Rd side of Strathfield Park. A great place for a light snack or meal during the warmer months, however may feel quite chilly during winter. The garden salad I ordered one Saturday was restaurant quality, and tasted fresh and crispy. Good menu choice, friendly staff. This cafe is recommended, however with the lack of nearby competition the prices may be slightly elevated.

[  ] Locating/Ease of Access: 10/10
Located in the heart of World Square, under the escalators leading up to the bar, this Cafe was a place we have passed a few times, but finally stopped in to eat. It was breakfast during the week.

[  ] Service/Wait Times: 10/10
The service was really quick, polite and friendly. We were served by a few people, all of which were accomodating.

[  ] Atmosphere/Feel: 9/10
The Cafe was tastefully decorated with artworks, all of which were for sale - it definitely suited the space. The layout definitely worked well.

[  ] Food/Drink: 10/10
The menu looked outstanding, and I ordered the food expecting to be underwhelmed. I was shocked when the food looked exactly like the menu, and tasted just as good. Very impressed!

[  ] Other: 10/10
49/50 - Overall the experience was incredibly pleasant. I would highly recommend stopping by if in World Square.
Friendly staff and quick service! It's a great cafe for family meals. It's fun to taste various menus. We're looking forward to this store. I recommend it!

It’s a lovely place to have dinner in the middle of the city. The staff were so wonderful and kind. The food was delicious and wanting to come back to try more of their dishes. Absolutely pleasure dining here.

A diverse variety of cuisines were presented at this lovely and welcoming restaurant. We were delighted to find each culture’s dishes made with the same experience and effort.

We ordered the bacon carbonara, wontons, and soft shell crab. They arrived quickly and the waitresses were friendly. The food was delicious and exquisitely prepared.

I would give this a 6 star rating if I could!!

This is a great place to sit down and get some food and nourishment. The server was very attentive and friendly. Wide selection of food available. We got the bolognese pasta, halloumi breakfast, light breakfast, waffle breakfast, veg omelette, and an avocado toast. Overall very enjoyable experience.

Malika Bakehouse is officially my new post-gym obsession. Everything was fresh, flavourful and beautifully made.

We had the sucuk & egg on a simit bagel (so good and perfectly balanced), the spinach, feta & ricotta börek which was flaky and comforting, and the baklava waffles… unreal. The Biscoff cookie and Turkish delights were the perfect sweet finish.

Highly recommend the iced pistachio coconut cloud matcha — creamy, nutty and not overly sweet.

Such a great mix of savoury and sweet options, generous portions and a welcoming vibe. Will definitely be back.

I had the steak sandwich with fries and it was absolutely delicious. The gozleme was fresh, light, and full of flavour, genuinely divine. You order at the counter and the service is friendly, quick, and on point. The food came out fast and everything tasted excellent. I wanted to try more but this was plenty and I probably didn’t need the chips. Malika Bakehouse was terrific, the space is airconditioned with comfortable seating and a really nice atmosphere. The coffee was amazing and hit the spot perfectly. I forgot to take a pic of the sandwich, but stuff it, the taste made up for it.

A beautiful little cafe in Surry Hills that’s super close to the light rail! The food was great and the drinks were also amazing! A big thank you to the staff Lee and Ayda, and to the kitchen staff Guzde and Tak for helping me and my guest feel welcome. I’d love to visit again whenever I’m in the area!

I come here almost every day for coffee and it’s always great. The staff are genuinely friendly and welcoming. The coffee is consistently excellent, and the borek was absolutely delicious. The café itself is very minimal and beautiful, with a really nice atmosphere. Definitely recommend.

Great service and friendly staff, but the food was bad. The burger bun was overly sweet, and the Eggs Benedict sauce was unpleasant. I couldn’t finish it and felt nauseous afterward. Nice ambience, poor food. And $80 for poor food is unacceptable

There are many hidden gem restaurants in Rosebery, but I was still blown away by the brunch at this place. The shop is spacious and bright, making it very comfortable to dine in. The service was excellent, and all the dishes recommended to us were delicious. Both the coffee and matcha were smooth and enjoyable. The ingredients are fresh, creatively combined, and taste absolutely fantastic. My favourite was their "Brûlée French Toast"—every bite was pure enjoyment. Highly recommend this brunch spot.

Nice, clean and spacious cafe, good for dating and gathering.

Cold brew is on the traditional high caffeine and dark roast style, not much acidity but more on the bitter and brown sugar roasting profile side.

The food is rather small proportioned but tastes good. Tom yum pasta will fit your assumption how it should be. If there is a slice of lime on the plate for the extra acidity can be even better, but only personal preference. Love the fluffy toast, guilty pleasure with the cheese and cream.

Had a lovely weekday brunch here. I had heard about, and was advised to try their French Toasts.
I tried the Brûlée French toast, and it was absolutely delicious. Melt in the mouth, soft and fresh. We had a fried chicken sandwich too which was tasty. Coffee is great too. They only have eggs for breakfast till 11am though, so consider going early if you like eggs for breakfast.
They have ample indoor and outdoor space to sit and tables are far apart enough to have a conversation. Service is good too.

The tomyum pasta is seriously delicious. It wasn't too spicy and it moorish. The crab croissant was made perfectly - crispy and plenty of filling. I felt the creme brulee signature French toast was abit on the sweet side but some people may find it perfect. I washed it down with delicious strawberry matcha (beats the coconut one). Plus it's dog friendly so bring it along. The only thing that could be improved is to do something about the flies that keeps flying around our dishes!

I came here with my girlfriend for lunch and was a bit disappointed by the experience. The man serving us was slightly rude, nothing out of the pocket but he presented himself as if he didn’t want to be there. He sat us outside and didn’t provide cutlery so we had to go inside to get some when we realised.

In regards to the food I ate the Thaibonara and my girlfriend had the signature crème brule French toast. I thought my pasta was very average, there was only 3 prawns and the flavours weren’t very strong in my opinion so it was just a bit average. The French toast was slightly disappointing, I was expected it to be quite soft but the bread was honestly a bit dry, and the flavours didn’t compensate enough so once we got past the top layer it just felt as if we were eating bread for $20+.

I wasn’t very pleased with our experience and will not be returning, but I think it is important to try new places, so please check them out, you might have a different experience and opinion to me :)

Nice and cozy cafe next to Green Square station. Will have some bonus if you are Popmart (or Labubu) lover.

Cold brew is made from espresso blend, got punchy, strong taste and very caffeinated (when you need it).  Size is generous for dine in, and it comes with a nice garnish. Matcha latte is defaulted with no sugar, which is kind of nice to enjoy the ‘all matcha’ flavour.

Chicken sandwich is delicious - bread is toasted just right with golden brown surface, cheese is perfectly just melted, chicken is tender. The flavour of ‘New Orlean’ marinate can be slightly stronger but in general I liked it quite a lot.

Finding a spot in Zetland that actually feels like a "local" can be tough with all the new apartments, but this little gem nailed it.

Honestly, the coffee here is on another level. You can tell they take their beans seriously, I ended up buying a bag to take home because the house roast was that good.

Perfectly balanced and zero bitterness.
What really makes the place, though, is the vibe. The owners and the staff are super good people; they’re actually friendly and remember your order instead of just rushing you through.

It’s rare to find a shop where the team genuinely seems happy to be there.
If you’re around Green Square or living in the 2017 area, definitely drop in.

Great drinks, even better people. 10/10.

Pleasant and good location corner cafe with good selection of drinks. It has changed ownership a few times, with a change of style. At the moment it has interesting choices of drinks, and about 8 hit food items with a great selection of pastries. Prices are a bit steep but nothing unusual for the area. Good location and close to Green Square. Problematic space inside it is a bit cramped
Cute cafe on a corner near Green Square station ☺️ The New Orleans Chicken was tender and seasoned nicely! Blueberry cream cheese bagel was buttery and soft 🫐🥯
I had strawberry matcha today. The taste is ok but could be improved. Not too sure what type of matcha they have but it’s no smell of matcha. I will try other drinks next time :)

We were in the city recently and found this cute little concept store and cafe ☕️ 😊👌@sorrythanksiloveyou ♥️in the middle of @westfieldsyd and they had great coffee, pastries and a lovely place to relax in quiet. Great to pop in if you find yourself in the city

After a long morning doing window shopping I came across the coffee shop  I chose this one instead of the other two in the upper levels because they are too small and crowded. Sorry, thanks, I love you is on the lower ground floor which gives that underground vibe plus has good space to sit.

Cool modern quirky coffee shop with charming staff polite & super friendly.  Coffee was also a big hit for me. Very smooth with flavour flavour flavour! Water was an added nice touch in a super cute bottle & glass

A hidden gem! It's actually a fashion accessories store that is also an amazing coffee joint in the basement of Westfield. Awesome single-origin coffee and friendly service. Decor is fantastic too. Definitely a great spot to take a break from shopping. Will definitely be my go-to coffee place in the city.

Secret spot to recharge the feet - at the back of a fashion boutique fixing sweet coffee and slow-brewed teas, with an eclectic instrumental playlist supplying national geographic world-cafe vibes in the background

Dulcet Cafe is such a lovely spot! Their cakes are absolutely amazing — beautiful, delicious, and made with so much care. Every bite is full of flavor, and they look as good as they taste. The café itself has a cozy, welcoming vibe, perfect for a relaxed coffee or afternoon treat. The staff are friendly and attentive, which makes the experience even better. Definitely a must-visit if you love great coffee and incredible cakes!

A party without a cake is called a MEETING! I brought this Christmas limited cake to a weekend staycation with friends and they were all impressed by this cake! The tastes and the shape are both stunning.
Actually this is my 5th year buying cakes, bakery and dessert from Dulcet. Glad to see they keep launching new products!
Their cakes are so good😋 I come here almost once a week!! The new Christmas cakes are great too!! I got the matcha one and it’s perfect for sharing with family ♥️

Dulcet provides finest quality of food and services. Comparing in markets, they always choose fine ingredients for a better balanced taste. Rather than put effort into marketing, they dive into professional development for providing trustworthy products. With high standards of quality and aesthetic taste, we always get wonderful customised cakes for special occasions and that was one of our best memories of the day.

The Mog Champ sends his regards.

It's a nice small cafe connected with Kinokuniya. Tried one of their strawberry cream cake here and it was good! It wasn't too sweet which is how we liked it, they have a few interesting cakes like the labubu mousse cake on display which seemed funny to look at. Thanks for the cake, Dulcet!

Mogliest Regards,
Your Mog Champ

Decent cafe for pancakes and brunch 🍓

Despite having pancakes in their name, I enjoyed their french toast more than the soufflé pancakes. Tried their savoury and sweet options and the food were quite yummy but I feel it’s quite expensive. The matcha french toast was made BEAUTIFULLY which paired well with the ice cream and strawberries. The mochi soufflé pancake tasted a bit dry and bland, same with the karaage pancakes. The chicken burger was very dense and was a big portion too.
Service was good and the atmosphere was clean and easy to talk in.

I have been here before when they were still somewhat new and a few times after covid.  The pancakes were not like before.  It has gone downhill since after covid but I thought maybe they were busy.  When I went today, it wasn't busy.  They tasted the same so flavour was still good.  However, they were not as fluffy anymore which was disappointing as they were supposed to be souffle pancakes which were supposed to be fluffy.  They were definitely fluffier when I first went.

We did not have to wait to get in and the store is decorated very cute. The portion is actually huge for a dessert so we had to share between 2 people.

We ordered the premium pancakes with additional vanilla ice cream and hojicha ice cream. The pancakes are super fluffy and delicious. The vanilla ice cream tastes just like full cream milk, I couldn’t taste any hint of vanilla though. The hojicha is very special and different with bittersweet taste of tea.

Definitely come back again to try different dishes.

The pancake was surprisingly good. I tried the Honey Apple and Earl Grey one. While the earl grey flavour wasn't as strong as I'd have liked, the overall flavour was well balanced. The texture was delightfully light and fluffy. The poached apples could be softer.
I also had a regular latte and a hojicha latte. The hojicha was flavourful, but neither were exceptionally good (though to be fair, Sydney has a strong coffee scene so tough to beat).

Atmosphere was nice. Not too busy for early lunch on a Saturday. It was a tad loud but could still hold a conversation.

Ordering if via a QR code so minimal service to review.

I live upstairs and come here very often and have always felt that the front desk service could be improved. Today I ordered a takeaway and waited for 20 minutes, but my order was still not ready. During that time, I was standing right next to the coffee counter and kept hearing the staff member with glasses complaining about making drinks, repeatedly saying things like “no more hot drinks, I don’t want to do this anymore.”

Eventually, I asked about my order and discovered she hadn’t even started making it. Only after I spoke up did she begin preparing it. This kind of attitude is really disappointing. If you choose to work here, at least please show some professionalism and respect towards customers.

Also today I asked if I can change the flavours in takeaway box and got told I can’t. In the past I could always pay to get different flavours. As it took me a long time to order the food, the girl went to serve other guests in between, I didn’t want to spend more time so I gave up and ordered what’s on the menu.

A spacious spot with both indoor and outdoor seating. Friendly service and food came out in a timely manner. I ordered the fish tacos — crispy battered flathead tucked into soft shells, loaded with fresh lettuce, smashed avo, a kick of Tabasco, and creamy sour cream. They were okay! The coffee, though, was really good.

The food itself was good overall and nicely presented — no complaints there. However, a few things took away from the experience.

The scrambled eggs were completely unseasoned, which was surprising given how simple that is to get right. A little salt and pepper would’ve gone a long way.

It was also a very hot day and the table water was warm — at the very least, some ice would’ve been appreciated.

Lastly, there was a sneaky 10% Saturday surcharge added to the bill. This wasn’t clearly communicated and is something I haven’t encountered before, so it felt a bit off-putting.

The total bill came to $77 for two meals, one coffee, and one juice, which felt a little steep considering the above issues.

There’s definitely potential here, but attention to these small details would make a big difference to the overall experience.

Will I return? Probably not.

love the interior of dirty red, they have plenty of outdoor seating and it just feels so welcoming inside. i sat at their bench, its a perfect spot to bring a journal or do some work on your laptop, and tried their jalapeño cheddar cornbread dish. it was amazing! naturally gluten free, packed with flavour and a wonderful chipotle sauce and i relished every bite of it. officially one of my fav cafes in sydney, i need to grab a coffee or a pastry next time.

Dirty Red in Glebe serves some of the best coffee you’ll find in Sydney’s Inner West. The café has a relaxed, welcoming vibe with a touch of Glebe’s signature charm — perfect for a quiet morning or a casual catch-up.

Their coffee is consistently excellent: rich, smooth, and perfectly balanced, with a deep aroma that shows real care in the roasting and preparation. Whether you go for a flat white, a long black, or a cold brew, each cup tastes fresh and thoughtfully made.
