The baristas clearly take pride in their craft and are always friendly and attentive, adding to the warm experience.

Pair your coffee with one of their delicious brunch options or pastries, and you’ve got a perfect start to the day. Dirty Red is a gem for anyone who appreciates great coffee and a relaxed, local atmosphere.

found this place while walking around glebe. got the chips and an aperol spritz. drink was nice and refreshing! i wasnt expecting much from the chips cos… theyre chips but they were surprisingly good! they had this nice garlicky seasoning which i was not expecting but im not complaining. its standard city cafe pricing (kinda exxy but maybe im just broke). server was quite kind and proactive! seating area outside was bright and welcoming. there was a good handful of people despite us going on a random monday. overall,, standard cafe in glebe (standards are high tho so thats good!)

Lunch for 3 | $48
Homemade simple comfort food, lovely service and atmosphere

We ordered the most popular menu items as recommended: aunty’s wonton soup, beef pho and steak sandwich.

Wonton soup was yummy, but not Hong Kong authentic level, mostly because noodles weren’t ‘al denté’ like they should be. Soup is more southeastern Asian style than special wonton soup. Wonton filling was really nice - prawn meat was fresh.

Beef pho was pretty good. Authentic good stock, but wish there were more toppings for the price.

Steak sandwich used scotch fillet, was fine too, but very cheap sliced toast. Portion was on the small side.

Overall a pleasant lunch, but there’s room for improvement. (Next time I would choose the dry noodles with wonton etc.)

Another gem on Botany Rd.

The room is spacious, chill and cool with the some art framed on the wall.

Auntie is very friendly and go through the menu with us. The menu consists of classic brunch like sandwich and toast and also Vietnamese egg (dry/soup) noodle with different toppings (pork, chicken, wonton and so on).

It’s nice bowls of noodles - the portion is not huge but it’s reasonable for the pricing. Won ton feels like house made - the filling doesn’t feel like high fat content compared to frozen packaged ones and each one is juicy and comes with one shrimp inside.

Sauce for dry noodle is nice and it’s the kind of sweet savoury you can expect from a good Viet restaurant.

The winner is the broth. Auntie told us there is no msg and is from chicken/pork one. The broth comes with dry noodle tastes clean and full of natural sweetness from pork bone and vegetable.

Will want to go back to try pho and other breakfast items.

We came across this cafe by chance after we went for an exercise class nearby. The owner is very friendly. The food is amazing! It looks good and tasted great. Totally recommend the wonton noodle and the pork dry noodle. The cafe is spacious and well decorated.
I’ve visited a few times now and the quality has been consistent. I’m a fan of the dry noodles and wontons here. Space is clean with plenty of seating. Auntie is welcoming and kind!

Run by husband and wife pair, Joeline and Minh - they’re such a lovely team who sit in quiet times hand wrapping wontons fresh for their soup.

A staple of the local community - I have been fed and loved by them every day I’m in the office.

Much appreciated. Allen of TZUKURI.

The best (and fastest served) cappuccino in Redfern, we kept coming back everyday!
The food was also very tasty and fresh, and the workers were all very nice as well. Only good things to say! :)

Also they have a fast and free WiFi!!

Redfern is plenty of nice cafes, but blue brew is hands down my favorite.

I missed from Sydney 3 months and I couldn’t wait to come back to have my fix. Both drinks and food are top tier and the staff is always extra nice - they never flinch at my crazy cappucino requests (extra choc, half decaf, soy 😂 - I’m so sorry!).

Thank you for always welcoming me and offering both amazing service and great coffee.

My favorites from the menu are the cold brew and the croissant eggs benedict. But any coffee tastes great. Really one level above any other cafe in the area.

Absolutely recommended!

Decent prices ~$20 average each dish, usual cafe fare dishes, executed very well, very smooth coffee, nice vibes inside and outdoor alfresco dining.very nice spot for catchups w/ friends. Bonus: no Sunday surcharge! (Unlike many other places).
The iced chocolate is a bit rich. Nice staff and outdoor seating. Okay drink. Would like to come back for food. The food looks nice

Small little cafe on the main street of Redfern. Interesting menu. Some safe options like your regular big breakfast and pancakes mixed with some trendy Asian inflluences dishes. Vegetarian friendly options. Good serving sizes. Nice chips. Quick service and flexible. We had a group of 6 people. They squeezed down quite quickly at two available tables while promising to allocated an extra table next to us when the customers finished. Paying the bill separately was allowed without any fuss.

Chill spot for matcha desserts and a relaxed sit down.

Ordered the Biscoff Matcha and it was enjoyable overall. The matcha flavour is present without being too bitter, and it pairs nicely with the sweetness from the Biscoff layers. The texture is soft and creamy, with a good balance between the matcha and cookie notes, though it does lean on the sweeter side.

The space itself is very minimal and clean, lots of light wood, simple seating, and a calm atmosphere. It’s not crowded or noisy, making it a nice place to sit and chat rather than a quick grab and go. Feels more like a quiet dessert café where you can take your time.

Tried this place for the first time today. We’ve wanted to come before but it was always busy with long lines, so we finally got the chance to try it.

The drinks were really good 💖 foamy, creamy, and not too sweet, which made them easy to enjoy. If you’re looking for good drink options, this place is a nice choice. The matcha was good.

The place is nice and cozy, and I love the interior and their bear logo too 🐻✨ cute!

I personally wasn’t a fan of the miso mushroom shokupan (just my preference), but my friend really liked it!

Came to try their matcha, we got the ichigo matcha (strawberry matcha) and matcha coconut. Both were pretty nice but felt the matcha coconut one was a bit on the bitter side. Ichigo matcha was a bit sweeter but the matcha taste could’ve been a bit stronger. We did enjoy it regardless and would definitely come back to try their other drinks and food!
The cafe itself is quite clean and aesthetic, service was friendly too.

I go here all the time, pretty much every week at this point.

They produce a solid matcha, it’s on the sweeter side but I love it. Coconut matcha cloud is the best one, and their coconut coffee cloud drink is also amazing.

The desserts are delicious as well, like the banoffee cake, a must try.

Can get pretty busy and there is barely any seating, but that’s fine since I normally get takeaway.

If you’re a matcha lover in Sydney, Chubby Cubby Cafe is your new paradise! I popped in to escape the sun and left completely obsessed. The Banana Oreo Matcha is a revelation—creamy banana pudding, velvety matcha sauce, and crunchy Oreo layers create a party of textures in every spoonful. The “Christmas Tree” toast, topped with fresh strawberries and matcha cream, is as beautiful as it is delicious, while the light matcha tiramisu and crisp matcha macarons are perfect finishes.

editorial_summary.overview: Bookstore with a courtyard cafe, offering brekkie and lunch, plus wine, cocktails and share plates.

reviews.text: Groovy bookshop with heaps of interesting books - but a bit pricey. Charlie, the bookshop cat is adorable and has his own postcards and bookmarks for sale. Nice looking courtyard cafe too.
This is one of my favourite places for coffee and snacks, or dinner and cocktails. Or even buying books! Love it all.
Bought a book, had one of the bowls, and had a great time sitting and doing work. Great spot to come alone or with someone else. Fantastic atmosphere and vibes. Highly recommend!

You would see it at first glance from the outside, but this bookstore is actually a cafe with seating both outside in the courtyard (covered), and also upstairs in the bookstore itself.
Great menu, great vibe and great staff. Awesome place for a longer session to sit back and have a drink or bite while enjoying a book.
Great second hand book selection, as well as a great cafe/courtyard. Nice environment to hang out in. And the home of Charlie the cat!

editorial_summary.overview: Down-to-earth outpost offering eggs, toast & other brunch dishes, plus coffee & desserts.

reviews.text:
I had an onion bagel with scallion cream cheese and it was so good. The cream cheese that came with the bagel was generous and I wanted to make the most of it. Hence, I got another plain bagel to smother it with the remaining cream cheese.

My friends each got bacon and egg bagel sandwich and lox bagel sandwich and they were delicious. They serve good coffee as well. I had flat white and it didn’t disappoint.

I enjoyed my dining experience here and would love to come back again to try more items on their menu. Their gelatos look interesting with a wide variety of flavors to choose from. Would love to try them as well.

A very cute cafe tucked away close to the Strathfield railway station. Since this was my first visit, i tried their recommended original cheesecake and it was worth it. They also have a nice illustration about how the cheesecake came to be. Very nice.

Just across Strathfield train station and right in front of the Uber waiting area is this inviting spot, The Smelly Cheesecake.

I tried their Mango Cheesecake, which combines mango, mascarpone, and New York cheesecake. It had the perfect balance of sweetness and creaminess: absolutely 5/5.

I also had their Ice Mango Green Tea Latte. At first, the green tea was quite strong, but after blending it all together, the mango and tea complemented each other beautifully. Another 5/5!

Great food, nice ambiance, and overall a really enjoyable experience. Will definitely come back.

I came here with my kids to enjoy coffee with some sweets. The lady who served was quite rude. She aggressively placed our drinks on the table. I used to come here quite often and I do not want to come back here again due to unsatisfactory service.

Great food, coffee and super friendly staff.

I ordered the Reuben bagel with a gluten free bagel, which is a joy in itself to be able to enjoy a gluten free bagel as it is a rarity. The Reuben was packed perfectly with pastrami while still being balance with all the other components.

The blue cheesecake is interest. It has a not over the top strong taste while still having that signature blue cheese taste. It’s not sweet tasting but more on the savoury side, it is more on the denser side. If it was drizzled with some honey it would have knocked it out of the park flavour wise.

The white Einspanner (iced coffee) was just right also, they altered the syrups for me so it is not too sweet.

The staff was super friend and the atmosphere is really calm and welcoming as you dine (or as some were there with laptops and tablets)

editorial_summary.overview: Mezzanine venue with booth seating overlooking a bookshop, for all-day breakfasts, cakes and crêpes.

reviews.text:
I’ve been a repeat customer, and it never fails to impress. 💚

The food is consistently excellent, the menu detailed, well thought out and extensive.
No matter what you order, the quality is there. Breakfast, lunch, dessert or just tea (not a coffee drinker) and cake - it always delivers (I don’t even have much of a sweet tooth)!

The location and setting is a real highlight - particularly if you are a people-watcher.
Dining on the mezzanine, surrounded by a great bookstore, gives a great atmosphere - a true booklover’s paradise.
Waiting for a table is usually quite quick, though on holidays it can be busy.
Portions are generous and good value for money; even simple dishes like an omelette, a chicken toastie or raisin toast far exceed expectations.

The service is speedy and friendly - a highlight.

I have tried a range of dishes over time - and everything has been delicious.
Tip: the first couple of booths near the entrance are quite small, so it’s worth asking for another seat if possible.

All up, this is a brilliant, slightly hidden gem in the city with a great vibe, excellent food, generous servings and a unique setting.

I’ll be back, again and again. 👍

Love the atmosphere here! Pity that we can’t study or work here… the Vienna (black) coffee had chocolatey notes, the cream was nice and cool and sprinkled with a tad bit of chocolate.

For the main meal I ordered chicken and veggie soup (comes with sourdough). It has potato’s, chicken (obviously), carrot and other veggies in it. It was warm and the veggies melt into your mouth. Though there were way too many green toppings- it all i could taste in every bite 🙂‍↕️…

The waitress was so lovely and accomodating. She had a kind smile and was polite and patient with me, suggesting options near to what I was trying to explain.
We arrived absolutely starving! The service was incredibly fast—honestly the quickest we’ve seen in Australia. The food was delicious, and our waitress was so lovely. Thank you for hospitality! ✨

Loved the Eastern European-style menu I don’t usually have; the food was amazing. I will come back to try another menu. The waitress was so kind and helped us choose, and the classic inside the building was great. Visit again 100%

This cafe is an experience on its own, situated on the first level,  ironicly past the cooking books.

We have been here previously, so came again for lunch today.

We had the Beef Piroshki, the Chicken Dumplings, mug of Flat White coffee, Tiramisu Iced Latte (didn't taste.much like Tiramisu), finishing off with a Biscoff cake with icecream! We rolled down the stairs contentedly once we were finished 😁.

The waitstaff in our area were both friendly and very attentive. We had our table cleaned before we sat down. The table was clean and not at all sticky, like some lesser establishments.

Unfortunately, the toilets (located near Australian History) were out of order.

Thanks Dymocks Cafe!

This place  doesn’t deserve a star , I went with my daughter yesterday at around 2pm to XS Gregory hills and ordered a big breakfast we sat inside at a corner table with a high bench next to it , we received our food and enjoyed it having a conversation and there was nothing wrong with the food until I was about to finish my food we saw a very big cockroach crawl from under the table we were eating at, we both looked at it in shock it crawled quickly to the top bench near our table that’s when I geabbed the table number and used it to kill the cockroach. We were both in shock and disgusted I felt like I just ate a whole plate of cockroaches, I felt like throwing up. I called the manager who was leaving the restaurant and told her, she said they just had the place sprayed the day before, which means they know they have cockroaches in the premises. I asked for a refund which she did she also offered a voucher to the value of the big breakfast which was $33. I gave my details as she asked for it, about 30 mins after I left the restaurant, I received a call from the owner I thought he was calling to apologise but he rang to attack me and criticise me and asked me questions because he was more concerned about how his staff were feeling! because I received a refund and a voucher. I explained that i don’t want the voucher but I did take the refund he then started acussing me and was very very rude. He wasn’t apologetic and was arrogant  and very rude. It’s not my first time there all the times I’ve been there the staff are friendly but this time I was actually eating with cockroaches. I explained how disgusting I felt and very unhygienic and dirty and then to be treated so rudely by the owner, I’m feeling traumatised, attacked for complaining and very upset by this whole situation.  I will never ever go there again and I don’t want to use the voucher they’ll probably put cockroaches or other insects in my food. Very unhappy and upset customer. Pics of the cockroaches attached..

By far the juiciest, most delicious burgers I've had yet. Also the fries are seasoned well but not overwhelmingly flavoured. My wife and a friend of mine enjoyed our stay too. It's relatively quiet for a roadside Cafe. A very peaceful vibe to the place. Good for a heart to heart.

My wife just ordered and have a look to what I received..
At least put something to cover the coffee..
I can’t understand how you give to uber driver like this without the coffee cup cover…
Bad customer service
Fantastic Cafe!  Amazingly food and service. Very accommodating staffs. Had the big breakfast today and the portion impressive! Highly recommended!
Went in today to grab a late breakfast with my kids . It’s a great cafe with generous portions at a good price . Staff were friendly aswell. I will be going back again

Vivace Café offers a decent option if you’re after a quick gelato fix without breaking the bank. The gelato itself is good enough. It's not the best you’ll ever have, but enjoyable and satisfying for the price. The big plus is that it’s reasonably priced, so it’s a solid choice when you want something sweet and simple without spending too much.

I have been coming here for ages, recently the acai has been filled extremely low (1/3 of my cup was filled with just strawberries and air)
Lovely service and staff tho!
Tiny portions and uneven granola-to-acai proportion don't buy their acai!!! This was $11

FREE Cockroaches in your food! Got a large oat cappuccino from here yesterday morning to go. Driving away in my van and notice a chunk of something in my mouth about halfway through the coffee. Pull over to spit it out and see it is a German cockroach which I had in my mouth. Have seen them running around the premises as well. Absolutely disgusting! Place should be shut down, pest controlled and deep cleaned before they are allowed to reopen.
there was an insect in my bubble tea so didn’t have it as I saw some bad reviews when i came to put a review.

Absolutely loved the experience here! The coffee was fantastic—smooth, rich, and perfectly brewed. What truly stood out was the exceptional customer service. The staff were warm, attentive, and went above and beyond to make sure everything was just right. Whether you’re a coffee enthusiast or just looking for a cozy spot, this cafe is a must-visit. I will definitely be back!

Matcha tasted very light and had lot of crumbs at the bottom. They gave me my matcha as soon as I ordered I was impressed by how fast they made it, however, it seems they didn’t whisked the matcha powder enough. Good service, nice cute little shop.

Came on the recommendation of a friend. Not usually a huge fan of bagels as they can often be dry, heavy and hard to chew but this was the opposite! Crunchy on the outside and super soft on the inside - it was toasted just perfect. They also spread some soft of jam instead so even the basic “egg & avo” tasted delicious! Definitely coming back to try the other bagels.
I stopped by and grabbed a matcha latte — the matcha flavor was rich, and the taste was not too bad.

Highly recommend this quirky little cafe. I ordered the cream cheese honey bagel, it was honestly the best bagel I’ve had so far. Also not forgetting to mention how good the coffee is.
Looking forward to coming back and having another delicious bagel.
Great coffee and a cozy atmosphere. The flat white was smooth and well-made, and the staff were friendly and efficient. A perfect spot to relax.

They only have drinks here but they made this literally Old TAB into something a bit homely and looking different from other places! You feel like your in someone’s home here. It’s got sum interesting memorabilia too,worth having a look if you want to see something unique
There are always many people enjoying the area which is shady and offers both indoors and outdoor service. They also take your order to you with a friendly smile.
Very good 👍 love my coffee here.
good coffee, Will definitely be back again.

Chill cafe next to central station and Haymarket light-rail/bus stop. All tables have QR codes to place an order to that table. The menu is super customizable down to the number of sugars and the temperature of the drink. We stayed here for an afternoon after checking out of our hotel before leaving for the airport and it was a very relaxing atmosphere with no problems. There are places to charge electronics, bar seats, tables, and couches. We ordered lunch here and the burger was pretty good too. Lunch plates come with fries, salad, and a drink. Would recommend!

Had a dirty chai and latte which was delicious and we were promptly served with water when we arrived however the noise around the area was horrible. There were loud sirens going off for the span of our visit. Not very busy but was very loud with chatter, maybe the sound moved more strongly here. Lots of seating available. Wifi available.

Calm atmosphere with a mixture of tables and sofas to sit on. Lovely pastries, especially the almond croissant. The coffee was good - we noticed it was significantly better quality when we had it to have in house rather than takeaway.

I’ve been coming to InCafe for over three years and it never disappoints! I’ve enjoyed breakfast, lunch and dinner here with friends – always great value, a good range of tasty dishes, and most importantly, the staff are warm and welcoming. Highly recommend!

This cafe is absolutely great! I've been here many times, and will always come back! It's like my go-to-place to destress and unwind. They don't provide a scenic view but instead a cozy work-vibe. I find it great because I can work on my paperworks, or just chill and watch in peace. Perfect for people who wants some space to just sit and relax. The place isn't noisy compared to other restaurants and cafes I've been to, the food is great and have value in them, they provide outlets for your gadgets to charge as well. Parking can be difficult because of it's location, thus I would recommend just taking the train as it's just beside Central station.

Absolutely love this place.
Avo toast is delicious, staff are very attentive and kind. Coffee is great. Only wish they had almond milk 🙏

Both batch brew and cap are top quality. Beans have a deep and distinct flavour. Owner/barista is unaffectedly good, can feel his passion in his work. Just good coffee made well. Regret that it took me so long to try this place.

I bought a "long black" over the weekend just before the cafe closed and by the time I got to my car realised my cup was only half full. Flavor of coffee was great, very smooth and tasty. However $5.50 for a "long black" which only lasted a few sips, very disappointing.

I've been here several times, there’s nothing wrong with this store except that it closes early (the store closes at 1pm). If you drink coffee in the morning, it's a good choice. Nice coffee and good vibes.
Nicely presented flat white and very friendly barista staff. The coffee is the type of my liking. I tried both take away and dine in both keep good quality.

Stumbled across Susu Lane at night and it turned out to be a really nice surprise. The store has a warm, modern feel with wooden tones, soft lighting, and plenty of clean seating both inside and outside. It’s actually a lot more spacious than it looks from the street, and the layout makes it easy to sit down and relax without feeling crowded.

The shaved ice itself was the good but not mind blowing. The portion is okay, the texture is super fine, and the toppings have good variety. The one I tried had a refreshing fruity flavour with chewy pearls and jelly that balanced everything really well. You can definitely finish it as one person.

They also have a small range of cakes and tarts in the cabinet. Presentation was neat and the variety looked good, though some trays were already empty which probably means they sell out quickly.

Prices are around the mid range for shaved ice in Sydney.

I purchased the original and mango tiramisu, and both were absolutely amazing — 10/10. I also tried the strawberry cold foam matcha and the tiramisu latte, which were just as delicious. The café is in a great location, not too crowded, with good indoor seating and a small but nice outdoor area. Unfortunately they were out of stock of a few items i was hoping to try. Prices were fair and definitely worth it for the quality. Highly recommend this place, and I’d love to return to try the bingsu next time.

Saw this place got some reviews on tiktok and wanted to try out. Ordered the strawberry foam matcha, it was nice, not too sweet, but could use a little bit more matcha. The tiramisu (original) is delicious, very rich with the coffee and cacso taste, the cream is smooth, not too fatty so it’s a balance combination. Very lovely staff I met this morning.

Probably one of the best tiramisu i had!! i love this newly open dessert store! Definitely perfect for dessert runs!
However, the iced matcha could have used more matcha since it’s a bit too milky for me! I usually like strong matcha taste so this iced matcha was 3/10 for me!

Lovely cafe spot in Central park avenue staff is very friendly and the shop has a great vibe for being newly opened. However, I found the matcha to be a bit too milky for my taste I would have preferred a stronger tea flavor.
